summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with worship and prayer, focusing on the theme of God's favor. He defined favor as God's blessing on our lives, emphasizing that it can take us further than a lifetime of labor and open doors we never knew existed. The message centered on five biblical keys to unlocking this divine favor. First, we must ask for it, as illustrated by the prayers of Jabez and Nehemiah. Second, we must pursue a life of humility, as God gives grace to the humble. Third, we must walk in righteousness, which means being clothed in Christ's righteousness and obeying God's commands, leading to a blessed life. Fourth, we must be faithful, staying committed to God and our responsibilities even in difficult times, as faithfulness finds favor. Finally, we must live generously, both toward God and others, understanding that we are stewards of all God has given us. Pastor Travis concluded by connecting God's ultimate generosity in giving His Son to the call for us to generously give our lives back to Him in surrender. The sermon was a practical and encouraging guide on how to position our lives to receive and walk in God's abundant favor.
mainPoints: We must ask for God's favor, following the examples of biblical figures who prayed specifically for God's blessing and guidance.
Walking in humility, righteousness, and faithfulness positions us to receive God's favor, as He blesses those who are obedient and committed.
Living a life of generosity, recognizing we are stewards of God's gifts, unlocks further blessing because God is generous to the generous.

versesMentioned.text: I have sought your face with all my heart; be gracious to me according to your promise.
And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.

Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, 'Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory! Let your hand be with me, and keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain.' And God granted his request.

Lord, let your ear be attentive to the prayer of this your servant and to the prayer of your servants who delight in revering your name. Give your servant success today by granting him favor in the presence of this man.'

Has not my hand made all these things, and so they came into being? declares the LORD. 'These are the ones I look on with favor: those who are humble and contrite in spirit, and who tremble at my word.'

The LORD will make you the head, not the tail. If you pay attention to the commands of the LORD your God that I give you this day and carefully follow them, you will always be at the top, never at the bottom.
Blessed are all who fear the LORD, who walk in obedience to him.
Surely, LORD, you bless the righteous; you surround them with your favor as a shield.
Good people obtain favor from the LORD, but he condemns those who devise wicked schemes.
Whoever can be trusted with very little can also be trusted with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much.
but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.
Let love and faithfulness never leave you; bind them around your neck, write them on the tablet of your heart. Then you will win favor and a good name in the sight of God and man.
In everything I did, I showed you that by this kind of hard work we must help the weak, remembering the words the Lord Jesus himself said: 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.'
Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: 1 Chronicles 4:10 (NIV) - 'Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, "Oh, that you would bless me..."'
Monday.action: Write down one specific area where you need God's favor and pray the prayer of Jabez over it today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I ask for Your blessing and favor in my life today. Enlarge my influence for Your glory and keep me from harm.

Tuesday.scripture: Isaiah 66:2 (NIV) - 'These are the ones I look on with favor: those who are humble...'
Tuesday.action: Intentionally put someone else's needs or interests ahead of your own in a conversation or decision.
Tuesday.prayer: God, help me to walk in humility today. Let my words and actions reflect a heart that puts others first and stands in awe of You.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 5:12 (NIV) - 'Surely, LORD, you bless the righteous; you surround them with your favor as a shield.'
Wednesday.action: Read one chapter in Proverbs and identify one command you can obey in your relationships or work today.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, clothe me in the righteousness of Christ. Help me to obey Your Word so that I may walk in Your blessing and protection.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 3:3-4 (NIV) - 'Let love and faithfulness never leave you...'
Thursday.action: Faithfully complete a task or responsibility you've been putting off, doing it as unto the Lord.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, strengthen my commitment. Help me to be faithful in the small things, trusting that You will entrust me with more.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:6 (NIV) - 'Whoever sows generously will also reap generously.'
Friday.action: Give something away today—your time, a gift, or an encouraging word—without expecting anything in return.
Friday.prayer: Generous God, thank You for giving Your Son for me. Help me to be a cheerful giver, stewarding all I have for Your kingdom.

Saturday.scripture: Luke 2:52 (NIV) - 'And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. Which key to favor (ask, humility, righteousness, faithfulness, generosity) did you grow in? Which needs more focus?
Saturday.prayer: Lord, like Jesus, I want to grow in favor with You and others. Examine my heart and show me how to better position my life for Your blessing.
